rxn_class_summary: Photoenzyme-catalyzed enantioselective [2+2] cycloaddition of indoles using a thioxanthone-based photosensitizer embedded in an engineered protein scaffold (CMP4.0)

key_intermediates.name: thioxanthone photosensitizer 3c triplet excited state (T1, nπ*/ππ* mixed)
triplet biradical from energy transfer to indole substrate 1a
CMP4.0·1a ground-state complex (π–π stacked at 3.9 Å, enantioface-selective)

electron_flow: Visible-light excitation of the protein-bound thioxanthone (3c) produces a long-lived triplet state whose nπ* character disfavors H-abstraction; triplet–triplet energy transfer to the π-stacked indole 1a generates an excited indole/biradical that undergoes enantioselective [2+2] cycloaddition within the chiral active site.
rate_determining_step: Unimolecular photochemical step on bound substrate (kcat linearly dependent on light intensity); Michaelis–Menten kinetics with KM = 36.69 µM and kcat = 11.28 min⁻¹

probe_reagents.name: Michaelis–Menten kinetic analysis
probe_reagents.observation: Saturation kinetics for CMP1.0 (kcat/KM = 209.47) and CMP4.0 (kcat/KM = 306.88 mM⁻¹ min⁻¹); kcat linear in light intensity → unimolecular photochemical RDS

probe_reagents.name: Photosensitizer comparison (CMP4.0 405 nm vs CMP4.0′ 365 nm vs free 3c 405 nm)
probe_reagents.observation: Bound 3c in CMP4.0 gives much higher yield than free 3c, confirming active-site catalysis

probe_reagents.name: UV-vis and photoluminescence spectroscopy
probe_reagents.observation: PL of CMP4.0 at 451 nm vs free 3c at 471 nm; quantum yield 17% vs 7%; longer τ2 (6144 ns vs 536 ns) indicating stabilized excited state in the protein

probe_reagents.name: Time-resolved emission decay (kinetic traces)
probe_reagents.observation: Double-exponential fit: CMP4.0 τ1=139.95 ns, τ2=6144.62 ns vs 3c τ1=2.95 ns, τ2=536.54 ns — protein matrix dramatically lengthens triplet lifetime

probe_reagents.name: X-ray crystallography (PDB 8ZF1) + docking
probe_reagents.observation: Two photosensitizers 5.8 Å apart; substrate 1a π-stacked at 3.9 Å with one face shielded, rationalizing enantioinduction

notes: Authors invoke energy transfer rather than SET; the nπ*-character triplet of thioxanthone is invoked to explain higher photostability vs benzophenone-based photoenzymes (which suffer H-abstraction via ππ* triplets). Alternative SET/radical-cation pathway is not explicitly probed by TEMPO/BHT trapping or EPR; mechanism inferred largely from photophysics, crystal structure, docking, and kinetics. Loading reduced to 0.25 mol% with >350 turnovers and ≥95:5 e.r.
